1927803898611290540:
Looking at the search results, I need to analyze the claims in this tweet about the arrest and threats against President Trump.

The tweet makes several claims that require fact-checking:

1. **The arrest of Ramon Morales Reyes**: The tweet presents this as a straightforward case of someone who threatened Trump being arrested.

2. **Reference to Butler, Pennsylvania shooting**: This refers to the confirmed July 13, 2024 assassination attempt.

3. **Claims about Comey calling for assassination**: This refers to Comey's controversial Instagram post.

4. **Call to "tone down rhetoric"**: This is presented as a response to these incidents.

The most significant issue with this tweet is regarding the first claim. According to multiple sources [4][7][13][14][15], the case against Ramon Morales Reyes later unraveled when it was discovered he had been framed by another person, Demetric Scott, who admitted to writing the threat letters. Key facts that contradict the tweet's implications:

- Morales Reyes doesn't speak or write English fluently [7][15]
- Handwriting analysis showed he didn't write the threatening letter [4][13] 
- Demetric Scott, who was facing trial where Morales Reyes was set to testify against him, admitted to writing the letters [13][14]
- Scott was charged with witness intimidation, identity theft, and bail jumping [13][14]

The tweet presents the arrest as definitively stopping a genuine threat, when in fact the person arrested was later determined to be a victim of a frame-up scheme.

**Community Note:**

Looking at this tweet about the Karen Read murder retrial from May 29, 2025, I need to check for any misleading information that would warrant a Community Note.

The tweet states the prosecution rested (accurate per sources [2][5][8]) and that "Read's defense team will have its turn to prove she didn't murder her boyfriend." This contains a fundamental misunderstanding of criminal law - the defense doesn't need to "prove" innocence. The prosecution must prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt.

Additionally, since this tweet was from May 29, 2025, I can provide important context about the ultimate outcome - Read was acquitted of murder charges on June 18, 2025 [6][9][4].

The defense doesn't need to "prove" innocence in criminal cases—the prosecution must prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt. 1. That he "voted to impeach" Trump
2. That he "agreed to endorse @NikkiHaley until you got scared and backed out"

From the search results:
- Tim Scott opposed both Trump impeachments [10]
- Scott endorsed Trump, not Haley, in the 2024 Republican primary after ending his own campaign [4][12]
- There's no evidence Scott ever agreed to endorse Haley

Looking at the search results, I need to fact-check the claims in this tweet about Khaby Lame's detention.

The tweet makes several claims that need contextualization:

1. **ICE detention claim**: This is accurate - Khaby Lame was indeed detained by ICE on June 6, 2025 at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas.

2. **"Far-left TikToker" characterization**: This appears to be misleading. According to multiple sources, Khaby Lame's content is described as "nonverbal and completely apolitical" - he's known for silent videos mocking complicated life hacks, not political content.

3. **"Most followers on TikTok"**: This is accurate - he has over 162 million followers.

4. **The context of detention**: The tweet implies this was politically motivated, but sources indicate he was detained for "overstaying the terms of his visa" after entering the US on April 30, 2025.

5. **Outcome**: He was granted "voluntary departure" the same day, which means he wasn't formally deported and can potentially return to the US in the future.

**Community Note:**

Lame was detained for visa overstaying, not political reasons. His content is apolitical, contradicting "far-left" claim. Looking at this tweet, I need to fact-check the key claims and provide relevant context about India-Russia oil trade and diplomatic developments.

The tweet makes several claims that I can verify from the sources:

1. India being the second-largest purchaser of Russian oil - This is accurate according to multiple sources [1][7][9][12]
2. Reference to Modi-Putin phone calls - There have been recent calls between them [3][6][17][18][19][20]
3. The connection between oil proceeds and war funding - This is supported by the sources

However, the tweet lacks important context about why India increased Russian oil imports (economic reasons due to steep discounts), India's consistent diplomatic stance on Ukraine, and the consequences India faces from the US over this trade.

India became Russia's 2nd largest oil buyer (35% vs <2% pre-war) due to steep discounts. Modi repeatedly stated India supports peaceful Ukraine resolution. US imposed 25% tariffs on Indian goods over oil trade. https://www.csis.org/analysis/guns-and-oil-continuity-and-change-russia-india-relations Add-ons: Looking at the search results, I can see that while the FBI did raid John Bolton's home on August 22, 2025, the timeline and nature of what was released doesn't match the tweet's claims.

The FBI conducted the raid on August 22, but court documents showing what was seized were released days later on Thursday, not immediately after the raid. These were redacted court filings listing seized items, not "images of classified documents" as the tweet suggests.

**Community Note:**

No evidence FBI released images of classified documents from Bolton's home on Aug 22. Looking at this tweet and the available evidence, I need to fact-check the claim that Bono made statements "despite not having any evidence."

From the search results, I can see that:

1. Bono did appear on Joe Rogan's podcast and made claims about USAID cuts causing deaths [1][4][7]
2. However, he was citing specific research by Boston University researcher Brooke Nichols [1][3][9][14]
3. This was published academic research with peer review, not unsupported claims [9][14]
4. The study estimated 332,553 deaths as of June 26, including 224,575 children [3]
5. While officials like Secretary of State Rubio disputed these findings [3], the existence of the research contradicts the claim of "no evidence"
6. The net worth figure is also slightly inflated - sources consistently cite ~$700 million, not $750 million [2][5][8][17]

The key misleading aspect is characterizing Bono's statements as having "no evidence" when he was actually referencing published academic research from Boston University.

Bono cited a Boston University study by researcher Brooke Nichols estimating 332,000+ deaths from USAID cuts. While officials dispute the findings, this was published research, not unsupported claims. https://bu.edu/articles/2025/mathematician-tracks-deaths-from-usaid-medicaid-cuts/ https://bu.edu/sph/news/articles/2025/tracking-anticipated-deaths-from-usaid-funding-cuts/ Add-ons: https://www.hindustantimes.com/world-news/us-news/bonos-appearance-on-joe-rogan-show-sparks-outrage-as-elon-musk-calls-him-liar-over-shocking-300-000-deaths-claim-101748716164629.html https://www.celebritynetworth.com/richest-celebrities/rock-stars/bono-net-worth/ https://www.medschool.umaryland.edu/media/som/news/news-logos/BU-researcher-warns-of-367,000-deaths-from-halted-USAID-programs_.pdf https://www.euronews.com/culture/2025/06/02/elon-musk-brands-bono-a-liar-and-an-idiot-over-usaid-cuts-criticism https://www.finance-monthly.com/bono-net-worth-2025-u2-fortune-revealed/ https://ph.ucla.edu/news-events/news/research-finds-more-14-million-preventable-deaths-2030-if-usaid-defunding https://www.imdb.com/news/ni65311273/ https://marketrealist.com/what-contributes-to-bonos-700-million-fortune-ex2/ https://www.bu.edu/articles/2025/mathematician-tracks-deaths-from-usaid-medicaid-cuts/ https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2025/08/historic-pocket-rescission-package-eliminates-woke-weaponized-and-wasteful-spending/ https://rfkhumanrights.org/person/bono/ https://www.schatz.senate.gov/news/press-releases/schatz-details-trump-administrations-destruction-of-usaid-deadly-consequences-that-followed-as-senate-considers-codifying-doge-cuts https://time.com/3987596/u2-activism-history/ https://www.bu.edu/sph/news/articles/2025/tracking-anticipated-deaths-from-usaid-funding-cuts/ https://www.kff.org/global-health-policy/u-s-foreign-aid-freeze-dissolution-of-usaid-timeline-of-events/ https://www.cbsnews.com/news/secretary-of-state-usaid-programs-canceled/ https://www.marca.com/en/lifestyle/celebrity-net-worth/2025/06/02/683e025b268e3ea82e8b4596.html
